
(3b)
Permutation test to see whether the essays were actually written by Mark Twain.
```{r}
xs <- c(0.225, 0.262, 0.217, 0.240, 0.230, 0.229, 0.235, 0.217)
ys <- c(0.209, 0.205, 0.196, 0.210, 0.202, 0.207, 0.224, 0.223, 0.220, 0.201)
boths <- c(xs, ys)
  
true_diff <- abs(mean(xs) - mean(ys))

# We will take a large number of permutations of the combined numbers assuming they come from the same distribution, and see where the true difference we calculated falls
gr_diff <- 0
for (i in 1:10000) {
  perm <- sample(boths, 18)
  sim_diff <- abs(mean(perm[1:8]) - mean(perm[9:18]))
  if (sim_diff > true_diff) {
    gr_diff <- gr_diff + 1
  }
}

cat("Permutation test statistic:",gr_diff / 10000)
```
